channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Hideo Kojima Wants OD to go ‘Beyond The Limit’ of Scariness
item.content:encoded:
Hideo Kojima has big plans for his upcoming horror title OD and wants to go ‘beyond the limit’ of scariness, pushing things further than any video game has before.

Initially announced in 2023, OD has big names in the cast, including Sophia Lillis, Hunter Schafer, and Udo Kier, and boasts involvement from Jordan Peele, who is behind films like Get Out and Nope.

While scary video games have tended to remain fairly tame and not push things too far into the nightmare realm, Kojima wants to go bigger and scarier than anyone before.

Kojima Wants OD to be ‘as Scary as Possible’

In an interview with Entertainment Weekly, Kojima revealed how many big companies turned down his proposal, who said he was “crazy” and that they “really don’t understand the concept,” before Xbox ultimately got on board.

Kojima explained he planned to “go beyond the limit of the ‘scariness’ that other games had reached,” with the title set as “a single-player game” that he wanted to make “as scary as possible.”

That may prove to be too much for some players, and Kojima is making OD more accommodating by devising a system that allows players to continue if things get too scary, though he “can’t say much more, because it’ll give too much of a hint on the system.”

  • Related: Asha Sharma Suggests XBOX Might Discover ‘The Next Kojima’

No release window for OD has been revealed as of yet, and it could still be a few more years in the making before gamers get thrust into the narrative nightmare.

Check out a teaser trailer for OD below.

Are you excited for Kojima to let fear run loose in OD? Let us know in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next, take a look at all the Ultimate Edition and pre-order bonuses for GTA 6, and don’t forget to subscribe to our newsletter.

item.title: GTA 6 Will Not Offer Early Access Incentive
item.content:encoded:
Rockstar Games has revealed all the details on pre-orders and the various editions of Grand Theft Auto 6 that will be available, and the lack of early access incentives stand out.

Releasing November 19, GTA 6 is arguably the most eagerly anticipated game of all time, and the wait times for the title have been extended by two release date delays.

While gamers around the world will be desperate to play GTA 6 as soon as they can, Rockstar Games is not offering any early access incentives.

GTA 6 Does Not Offer Early Access

In fresh details published by Rockstar Games regarding pre-order bonuses and editions of GTA 6 that will be available, there is no mention of an early access period as one of the bonuses.

Such incentives have become standard practice in the industry, offering players who splash out on a premium edition a chance to play the game early, ranging from a few days to as many as 10 days.

GTA 6 won’t be following that norm, however, and regardless of whether you purchase the Standard Edition or splash out on the Ultimate Edition, the game will release on the same date for everyone.

It shouldn’t come as too much of a surprise, however, as Rockstar has not offered such early access incentives for full games in the past, though there have been early access periods for specific content.

Services like GTA+ have routinely offered the first opportunity to grab in-game content like new vehicles and weapons, but this is a far cry from opening the doors early for a full release like GTA 6.

Rockstar doesn’t have any need to offer early access; in truth, as the game will sell like hotcakes regardless, though they certainly could have squeezed a few extra bucks out of players with the offer.

Check out the latest GTA 6 trailer below.

Which edition of GTA 6 are you planning to purchase? Let us know in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more GTA 6, take a look at all the Ultimate Edition and pre-order bonuses for GTA 6, and don’t forget to subscribe to our newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: GTA 6 Has Practically No Loading Screens on PS5
item.content:encoded:
One thing the Grand Theft Auto series has become iconic for, amongst many other things, is the beautiful and artistic loading screens. I’m not sure how much of this we’re getting with GTA 6, as it seems there will be next-to-no loading times.

Technology is constantly evolving, and it’s for the best. If you went back and played GTA 3 on a PS2 right now, you’d likely be mortified by the long waits. Now, with state-of-the-art consoles, GTA 6 might be about to remove loading screens pretty much altogether.

Loading Screens Could be a Thing of the Past in Grand Theft Auto 6

A detailed Sony PlayStation blog about GTA 6 has revealed a lot of things, but one of the most eye-catching details that caused me to raise an eyebrow, The Rock style, was a comment about the game’s loading times.

The blog said:

‘Grand Theft Auto VI also leverages the PS5’s ultra-high speed SSD, enabling you to experience the expansive world of Leonida with near-instant load times.’

An interesting point I noticed here is that it doesn’t mention ‘PS5 Pro’ throughout.

  • More Insider Gaming: GTA 6 Physical Editions Won’t Include Discs, Just Download Codes

Obviously, you’d think the game will run the best on PS5 Pro, and that’s how you’ll get the most reduced loading time and wait.

However, it doesn’t say this. This means there’s a good chance that if you’re playing GTA 6 on a base edition PS5, you also won’t be hanging around twiddling your thumbs too much.

Quite simply, Sony claims GTA 6 plays ‘Best on PS5.’

Sony PlayStation exclusives have done an amazing job with this. The Spider-Man games and Ghost of Yotei get you into the game almost instantly after booting them up, with Marvel’s Spider-Man 2’s fast travel system being pretty much instantaneous.

  • More Insider Gaming: GTA 6 Locks Five Single-Player Stores Behind $100 Price-Tag

Fingers crossed, all PS5 owners will get to experience ‘near-instant’ loading, and it could be the end of the era of GTA loading screens.

Cast your mind back to GTA Trailer 2 to get you excited.

Which edition will you be buying? Let us know through the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, check out the interesting find that GTA 6 is a ‘Single-Player Experience’ with no mention of GTA Online, and don’t forget to sign up for our Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: GTA 6 is a ‘Single-Player Experience’ With No Mention of GTA Online
item.content:encoded:
Fresh details regarding Grand Theft Auto 6 have emerged, including one particular line that could be telling for the future of GTA Online.

Releasing November 19, GTA 6 is set to follow on from the vast success of its predecessor, GTA 5, which has made Rockstar Games millions through the GTA Online mode.

Initially released as a mode accessible through GTA 5, GTA Online has evolved to have its own launcher, and while Rockstar has remained quiet about the online offerings in GTA 6, new details are telling.

GTA 6 a ‘Single-Player Experience’

On the PlayStation website, a series of FAQs covers significant topics about the game, with one of the questions with a response being: Does Grand Theft Auto VI have any multiplayer modes or features?

A single one-sentence reply has sent shockwaves through the community: “Grand Theft Auto VI is a single-player experience.”

While this doesn’t mean there won’t be any online offerings with GTA 6, the continued absence of the mode from marketing and the radio silence overall indicate that it could be entirely separate from GTA 6 itself.

  • Related: GTA 6 Locks Five Single-Player Stores Behind $100 Price-Tag

In fact, Rockstar has even gone as far as offering single-player exclusive stores for those who purchase the Ultimate Edition of GTA 6, and again did not mention anything about GTA Online.

With several months until the release of GTA 6, however, there’s still plenty of time for Rockstar to life the lid on the future of GTA Online, and the latest update of the mode could be telling.

In July, a new heist is coming to GTA Online for the first time in six years, and there’s plenty of interest on whether that will lead into a narrative taking the game into Vice City and the state of Leonida in GTA 6.

Check out the latest trailer for GTA 6 below.

What do you think Rockstar Games plans to do with online in GTA 6? Let us know in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more GTA 6, take a look at all the Ultimate Edition and pre-order bonuses for GTA 6, and don’t forget to subscribe to our newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: GTA 6 Physical Editions Won’t Include Discs, Just Download Codes
item.content:encoded:
Amid the myriad of details that Rockstar has revealed for Grand Theft Auto 6, it’s been confirmed that physical editions won’t include a game disc. Instead, buyers will find a download code that must be redeemed on their console.

Launching November 19, GTA 6 is undoubtedly one of the most anticipated games of all time, with two editions set to be available: Standard and Ultimate. Sadly, neither will include a physical disc.

It’s now been confirmed that no matter which version of GTA 6 players pre-order, physical editions will only include a download code. Rockstar is hardly the first studio to take this approach, but for a game of this scale and significance, many fans were hoping to own it as an actual piece of physical media.

That said, some have theorised that Rockstar is doing this to prevent players from accessing the game early. Physical pre-orders can sometimes be shipped before release day, and if a disc-based copy lands early, players may be able to jump in ahead of launch, aside from any required day-one patch.

  • Related: GTA 6 Locks Five Single-Player Stores Behind $100 Price-Tag

With GTA 6 set to be a cultural phenomenon, Rockstar will likely want to maintain control over the launch and prevent early leaks from fans who receive the game before everyone else. It’s a tough blow for physical media collectors, but with the Ultimate Edition including several digital bonuses, download codes were perhaps always likely.

It’s a bummer that there’s no physical disc, but it hardly kills the hype for the game. If you need a little pick-me-up, check out the latest trailer for GTA 6 below:

Were you hoping to have GTA 6 on disc? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more GTA 6 news, read about the cost of the base game, and don’t forget to sign up for our Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion
